The Li-7(P,N) reaction with proton energy
fixed at 6 keV above the reaction threshold.
.Discs, 1.5 cm in diameter, 5 or 3 mm thick, uranium
or plutonium samples in 0.2 mm thick stainless steel
boxes. Uranium sample enriched to 93 percent U-235.
.Tantalum discs of same geometry for background
Time of flight techniques with flight paths
discrimination located at 30 and 60 degrees symetri-
cally to the incoming proton beam.
.Detectors were energy calibrated by the T(P,N), the
D(D,N) and the T(D,N) reactions at different energies
.Detectors were efficiency calibrated up to 5 MeV by
comparison with a directional counter and at 14.6 MeV
using the associated particle coincidence techniques
.Fission neutron spectrum was fitted to a maxwellian
.No correction for flux attenuation and multiple
